unmapped_findings: The addition of 1 wt% Al leads to a 1.3% reduction in density and a 2% reduction in Young's modulus
Density reduction formula for ferritic steels: ρ_ferritic (g/cm³) = 7.874 - 0.098 (wt% Al)
Density reduction formula for austenitic steels: ρ_austenitic (g/cm³) = 8.15 - 0.101 (wt% Al) - 0.41 (wt% C) - 0.0085 (wt% Mn)
Critical Al content > 7% and C > 0.7% required for intragranular κ'-carbide precipitation
Al extends the δ-ferrite phase field and suppresses γ single phase region
Mn restrains κ phase formation while Al restrains formation of M₃C and M₂₃C₆ carbides
Processing challenges include: nozzle clogging during continuous casting, surface oxide formation, cracking during hot/cold forming, and reduced solidus temperatures
Specific stiffness comparison shows low density steels are not higher than HSLA steels and aluminium alloys
Strain hardening mechanisms include TWIP, TRIP, MBIP (microband induced plasticity), DSBR (dynamic slip band refinement), and SIP (shear band induced plasticity)
Impact toughness of solution treated austenitic steels is slightly lower than Cr-Ni stainless steels but higher than conventional high strength steels
Corrosion resistance is not improved compared to conventional high strength steels; coating is necessary
Weldability issues include parting in weld centreline, lower hardness in parting, peak hardness in HAZ, and reduced creep rupture life in weldments
Fatigue behavior data is conflicting and insufficient for definitive conclusions
Formability data is limited; hole expansion ratio comparable to DP600 but bending angle and dome expansion lower than conventional AHSS

metadata.title: Current state of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels

process_sequence.others: Rolled to 2-5 mm thickness; reheating between passes may be required to avoid cracking
Alternative to direct cooling or water quenching to room temperature
For ferritic and duplex steels; for austenitic steels prior to solution treatment

process_sequence.cooling_medium: Water, oil or other quench media
process_sequence.others: Performed in single austenite phase area
For age-hardenable austenitic steels to produce κ'-carbide precipitation hardening

others.note: For ferritic steels: cold rolling and recrystallization annealing at 700-1000°C required. For duplex steels: intercritical annealing at 700-900°C followed by austempering.

structure.overall_structure: Four categories: (1) Ferritic steels (δ-ferrite with possible A2, B2, DO3 ordering), (2) Ferrite-based duplex steels (δ + γ), (3) Austenite-based duplex steels (γ + δ + κ'), (4) Austenitic steels (γ with κ'-carbide precipitates)

phases_present.morphology: Elongated band-like structures, columnar in as-cast state

grain_structure.texture: Random with higher intensity of Cube fibre

precipitates.distribution: Along original δ-ferrite grain boundaries during slow cooling

phases_present.morphology: Equiaxed grain structure with annealing twins

precipitates.distribution: Along austenite grain boundaries, initially discrete then continuous

phases_present.morphology: Elongated stringers parallel to rolling direction
Fine stringers along grain boundaries or shear bands

grain_structure.grain_size_distribution: Bimodal structure with coarse δ-ferrite and fine austenite

precipitates.distribution: At δ/γ interfaces or within transformed regions

phases.coherence: Coherent, cube-on-cube orientation relationship [100]κ//[100]γ, lattice mismatch <3%

defect_interaction.quality_description: Glide plane softening due to short-range ordering leads to planar glide
Shearing of nano-sized κ' carbides by dislocations, contributing to planar slip
Dislocations pile up or bow out at B2 phase boundaries, particles not sheared

others.phase_evolution: γ → γ' + γ'' (spinodal) → γ' + L1₂ (SRO) → γ' + κ' during aging

interface.phase_evolution: Sequence: Spinodal decomposition → SRO formation → κ'-carbide precipitation → possible transformation to equilibrium κ + DO₃/B2 + β-Mn at prolonged aging

corrosion_resistance.passivation_behavior: Not improved compared to conventional high strength steels; inferior to 304 stainless steel

comparative_performance.benchmark_alloy: High Mn TWIP steels, conventional HSLA, DP600, 304 stainless steel

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure observation, precipitate identification, dislocation structure analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: κ-carbide crystal structure, L1₂ ordering, planar dislocation glide structures
κ'-carbide growth features, lamellar colony formation

characterization_methods.purpose: Chemical composition analysis at atomic scale
characterization_methods.key_findings: Off-stoichiometric composition of κ-carbide with depletion of C and Al

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase diagram calculation and thermodynamic modeling
characterization_methods.key_findings: Phase equilibria predictions, deviation from experimental data at high Mn/Al contents

computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Calculated phase diagrams compared with experimental data; deviations observed at high Mn (>20%) and high Al contents requiring improved databases
